LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AUG-11-2020 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT SAMSARA NETWORKS INC.'S (1) PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF PLAINTIFFS INDIVIDUAL CLAIMS AND DISMISS THE CLASS CLAIMS AND (2) MOTION TO STAY. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE CORRECTED LANGUAGE STATED BY THE COURT AT THE OUTSET OF ARGUMENT AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND DISMISS AND MOTION TO STAY ARE GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S PAGA CLAIM IS STAYED, PLAINTIFF'S INDIVIDUAL CLAIMS ARE COMPELLED TO ARBITRATION, AND PLAINTIFF'S CLASS CLAIMS ARE DISMISSED. (CODE CIV. PROC. SECS 1281.2, 1281.4.) ORDER SIGNED. (SEE SIGNED ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR#5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

Defendants (1) Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration Of Plaintiffs Individual Claims And Dismiss The Class Claims And (2) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-11-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. MAX W. GAVRON, Esq. of Diversity Law Group, APC (Tel: 213-488-6555) for plaintiff Guy Blaz Hanley;
Atty. TRITIA MURATA, Esq. of Morrison & Foerster LLP (Tel: 213-892-5200) for Defendant
Samsara Networks Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SAMSARA NETWORKS INC.'s (1) Petition To Compel Arbitration Of Plaintiffs Individual Claims And Dismiss The Class Claims And (2) Motion To Stay.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the corrected language stated by the Court at the outset of argument as follows: Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and dismiss and motion to stay are granted. Plaintiff's PAGA claim is stayed, Plaintiff's individual claims are compelled to arbitration, and Plaintiff's class claims are dismissed. (Code Civ. Proc. Secs 1281.2, 1281.4.)

(See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Order signed.
